title: Monsoon Update – Heavy Rain Alert Issued for These States in Next 48 Hours, Know the Forecast - Times Bull
article_text:
New Delhi: The next three days are unlikely to be easy, as weather conditions are expected to remain severely unsettled. High-altitude states may witness spells of rain accompanied by snowfall, leading to expectations of a significant drop in temperature levels.
In Uttarakhand, late-night snowfall and light rain in the hilly regions have caused temperatures to plummet below the freezing point. Similar conditions prevail in Himachal Pradesh and Kashmir. Weather conditions also remain adverse in South India, creating a precarious situation. According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), rain alerts have been issued for several states across the country. Read on below to find out what the weather will be like in specific regions.
According to the Meteorological Department, the weather in the high-altitude areas of Kashmir is extremely cold due to snowfall and rain, causing residents to feel the biting chill of winter. Furthermore, a blanket of snow currently covers most parts of the valley. The skies are expected to remain overcast, and weather conditions are anticipated to remain changeable for the next several days.
The Meteorological Department has issued an avalanche alert for the high-altitude regions of Sikkim’s Gangtok and Pakyong districts. According to an advisory issued by the Sikkim State Disaster Management Authority, there is a possibility of avalanches occurring within the next 24 hours in areas situated above an altitude of 3,500 meters. Most parts of this Himalayan state could experience heavy rainfall starting Friday morning.
According to the Meteorological Department, weather conditions in Delhi are likely to take a turn for the worse once again. The IMD has forecast the presence of dense cloud cover and the possibility of rain accompanied by gusty winds. Additionally, the department has predicted thunderstorms, lightning strikes, and hailstorms.
According to the Meteorological Department, rain alerts have been issued for various locations across Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, and Puducherry. Apart from these states, rain accompanied by thunder and lightning is also expected in Gujarat, Maharashtra, and Telangana.
Vipin Kumar is An experienced journalist with 8 years in the media industry, having worked with prominent news platforms including Dainik Jagran and News24. Currently serving at Timesbull.com for almost…

title: Kalbaisakhi storm alert: Thunderstorms, lightning & strong winds to lash Odisha for 7 Days - kalingatv.com
article_text:
Kalbaisakhi storm alert: Thunderstorms, lightning & strong winds to lash Odisha for 7 Days
The Meteorological Department has forecasted Kalbaisakhi (nor’wester) thunderstorms across Odisha for the next seven days which will bringing rain.
By Devanshi Patel On Mar 22, 2026
 Share

Bhubaneswar: The Meteorological Department has forecasted Kalbaisakhi (nor’wester) thunderstorms across Odisha for the next seven days which will bringing rain, lightning, and gusty winds to several parts of the state.

According to the reports, multiple districts are likely to experience thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and rainfall. Wind speeds during these events may range between 30 to 40 km/h, while stronger gusts are expected in some areas.

An Orange warning has been issued for Mayurbhanj and Balasore districts for today. These regions may witness intense thunderstorm activity, with wind speeds reaching 50 to 60 km/h, along with lightning and heavy rain.

Additionally, a Yellow warning has been issued for ten districts that are Sundargarh, Keonjhar, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapara, Kalahandi, Kandhamal, Koraput, Nabarangpur, and Malkangiri. These areas may experience moderate thunderstorms with lightning and rainfall.

For tomorrow, Mayurbhanj and Keonjhar districts remain under a Yellow alert, with expected wind speeds of 30 to 40 km/h along with rain and lightning.

The weather department has also predicted light to moderate rainfall across 14 districts covering coastal, northern, and southern parts of  Odisha.

The forecast indicates that such weather conditions are likely to continue across various parts of the state until March 28.

Authorities have advised residents to remain cautious during thunderstorms, particularly due to the risk of lightning strikes and strong winds.

title: Heavy Rain Alert: Heavy rains expected on March 22, 23, 24 and 25; Meteorological Department issues alert for these states - informalnewz
article_text:

Heavy Rain Alert: Heavy rains expected on March 22, 23, 24 and 25; Meteorological Department issues alert for these states
By
Shyamu Maurya

0

Heavy Rain Alert: Heavy rains expected on March 22, 23, 24 and 25; Meteorological Department issues alert for these states

dress
Books
Dresses

Heavy Rain Alert: The pre-monsoon season has picked up pace in the country. As a result, the Meteorological Department has issued an alert for heavy rain in several states on March 22, 23, 24, and 25.

Add informalnewz.com as a Preferred Source







Heavy Rain Alert: Since the pre-monsoon season arrived in the country, the weather has changed. The 2025 monsoon season saw abundant rainfall across the country, and rainfall continued in many states even after the season. Now, in 2026, the pre-monsoon has begun to show its effect, leading to a renewed rain cycle in many states. The pre-monsoon season has gained momentum in the country. Consequently, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a Heavy Rain Alert for several states on March 22, 23, 24, and 25.

Northwest India

The pre-monsoon season has gained momentum in northwest India. The Meteorological Department has issued an alert that heavy rainfall will occur in many areas of Rajasthan, Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Punjab, Haryana, Himachal Pradesh, Jammu and Kashmir, and Ladakh on March 22, 23, 24, and 25. During this period, there is also an alert for light rain in many places, along with strong winds with speeds of 40-70 kilometers per hour, lightning, and hailstorms in some areas.

Western India

The pre-monsoon season has also picked up pace in western India. The Meteorological Department has issued an alert warning that heavy rain will occur in Central Maharashtra and Marathwada on March 22, 23, 24, and 25. Thunderstorms and strong winds are also likely during this period.

Eastern and Central India

The pre-monsoon has also gained momentum in eastern and central India. The Meteorological Department has issued an alert that on March 22, 23, 24, and 25, there will be heavy rains in Madhya Pradesh, West Bengal, Sikkim, Odisha, Bihar, Jharkhand, Chhattisgarh, and Vidarbha, with light rain in some areas. During this period, there is also an alert for strong winds of 50-80 kilometers per hour, dust storms, lightning, and hailstorms in some districts.

Northeast India

The pre-monsoon has also gained momentum in northeastern India. The Meteorological Department has issued an alert for heavy rains in Assam, Mizoram, Meghalaya, Tripura, Manipur, Arunachal Pradesh, and Nagaland on March 22, 23, 24, and 25. There is also an alert for strong winds of 30-50 kilometers per hour and thunderstorms during this period.

South India

The pre-monsoon season has also picked up pace in South India. The Meteorological Department has issued a heavy rainfall alert for Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, coastal Andhra Pradesh, Mahe, Puducherry, Yanam, and Karaikal on March 22, 23, 24, and 25. Strong winds with speeds of up to 30-50 kilometers per hour are also expected in many areas during this period.
